_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 5 evidence-level study-design tiers used by backed_clinical_studies (197 entries). Determines the scoring multiplier for clinical-evidence bonuses. NOTE: ingredient_interaction_rules.json uses a DIFFERENT field also called `evidence_level` containing strength values (established/probable/theoretical) — that is a separate concept and warrants its own evidence_strength_vocab in a future sprint.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for evidence-tier labels in backed_clinical_studies. The `multiplier` field aligns with scoring_config.json effect_direction_multipliers.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/backed_clinical_studies.json `evidence_level` field. Multiplier weights: scripts/config/scoring_config.json. Flutter consumer: lib/core/constants/severity.dart `EvidenceLevel` enum (legacy mapping; future migration target).

field_contract.id: Stable kebab-case identifier (matches source-data hyphenation, e.g. 'product-human').
field_contract.name: User-facing label.
field_contract.multiplier: Numeric multiplier applied to the study's score contribution (matches scoring_config.json).

_metadata.future_split_note: ingredient_interaction_rules `evidence_level` (established/probable/theoretical/limited/moderate/no_data) should split into evidence_strength_vocab.json — different concept (qualitative grade vs study design).

evidence_levels.name: Product-level human RCT
evidence_levels.notes: Randomized controlled trial conducted on the actual finished product. Strongest study-design tier — directly applicable to the marketed formulation.

evidence_levels.name: Branded ingredient RCT
evidence_levels.notes: Randomized controlled trial on a specific branded raw material used in the product (e.g. KSM-66 ashwagandha). Strong because the trial used the same supplier-batched ingredient.

evidence_levels.name: Generic ingredient human study
evidence_levels.notes: Human study on the generic ingredient (not branded). Applicable but less specific to the formulation.

evidence_levels.name: Probiotic strain clinical study
evidence_levels.notes: Clinical trial on a specific probiotic strain used in the product. Strain identity is essential for probiotic evidence transfer.

evidence_levels.name: Preclinical evidence
evidence_levels.notes: Animal model or in-vitro evidence only. Mechanistic plausibility but no human confirmation. Used for context, not for primary scoring.
